novel_features.name: Watchlist management
novel_features.description: Track a set of events locally so one sync re-fetches all their current prices.
novel_features.rationale: TicketData has no list or browse endpoint, so the tracked set can only exist in the local store this CLI owns.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this first: everything downstream (board, drift, stats, compare) reads the watchlist the CLI syncs.

novel_features.name: Watchlist price board
novel_features.description: One sortable table of every watched event: get-in price, N-day change, forecast direction, and where today sits in its own history.
novel_features.rationale: Joins local snapshots and price history across all watched events in one view no single TicketData call returns.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use it for a whole-watchlist snapshot; for what changed since last sync use drift, for one event's distribution use stats.

novel_features.name: Drift and target alerts
novel_features.description: Diff the two most recent snapshots per watched event, flag floors that moved past a threshold, and fire price-target hits.
novel_features.rationale: A temporal diff over locally stored snapshots; TicketData exposes no what-changed-since call.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use it for what moved since the last sync and for buy-when-it-hits-my-price alerts.

novel_features.name: History stats and best time to buy
novel_features.description: Historical low/high, median, current percentile, volatility, and the weekday the floor is typically lowest, from the local price series.
novel_features.rationale: Distribution and seasonality math over hundreds of stored points; the site shows only a chart and one forecast number.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use it to judge whether today's get-in price is actually low for one event and which day tends to be cheapest.
novel_features.group: Price intelligence the site chart hides

novel_features.name: Cross-event comparison
novel_features.description: Rank multiple watched events, or all of one performer's watched events, by get-in price and percent change.
novel_features.rationale: Ranks across multiple locally stored event rows; TicketData has no multi-event endpoint.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use it to pick the cheapest city or date; for a single event's own history use stats.
novel_features.group: Price intelligence the site chart hides

novel_features.name: Best-zone opportunity finder
novel_features.description: Rank an event's zones by current get-in price and by each zone's drop versus its own history to surface the underpriced section.
novel_features.rationale: Cross-zone opportunity ranking from the stored per-zone series; the site shows per-zone charts but never ranks them.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use it to pick a section by value; for the plain section-name catalog use events sections.
novel_features.group: Price intelligence the site chart hides

novel_features.name: Offline multi-result search
novel_features.description: Local full-text search across synced events, performers, and venues, returning multiple matches.
novel_features.rationale: TicketData's resolvers return only a single best match; local FTS returns the whole match set.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use it to browse offline matches; for the single canonical resolve of a name use performers search or venues search.

narrative.headline: Every TicketData price signal for any event, performer, or venue, plus a local price-history store for trend math, drift alerts, and cross-event comparisons no ticket tool ships.
narrative.value_prop: TicketData tracks each event's get-in price (the lowest all-in resale price across marketplaces), its full price history, a forecast, and 3/7/14/30-day change. This CLI exposes all of it as scriptable, agent-native JSON and keeps the raw price series in local SQLite, so `stats` can compute historical lows and volatility, `drift` can alert when a floor drops to your target, `board` can rank your whole watchlist, and `compare` can pick the cheapest date, none of which the website surfaces.
narrative.auth_narrative: No account or API key. TicketData's public data API is open; this CLI reads it directly.

quickstart.comment: Confirm the CLI can reach the TicketData data API.
Resolve a name to its canonical performer and stats.
Get an event's current get-in price, forecast, and N-day change.
Track the event so sync starts building its local history.
See the historical low, percentile, and best day to buy.

troubleshoots.symptom: A command returns an empty board or 'no data' for stats/drift/compare
troubleshoots.fix: Run `ticketdata-pp-cli sync` first; these read the local store, which is empty until you watch events and sync.
Retry; the CLI ships a Chrome-fingerprint HTTP transport that clears it. Persistent 403s usually mean a transient challenge, not a block.
The event id no longer exists on TicketData. Re-resolve it with `performers search` then look up the performer's current events on the site.

narrative.when_to_use: Use this CLI when you need TicketData's get-in price, price history, forecast, or N-day change for specific events, performers, or venues, and especially when you want to track a set of events over time, compute historical price stats, alert on price drops, or compare events, all offline and scriptable. It is ideal for agents answering 'is this ticket cheap right now', 'when should I buy', or 'which of these dates is cheapest'.
anti_triggers: Do not use this CLI to buy, sell, or list tickets; TicketData is a price tracker, not a marketplace. Use the marketplace buy links from `events get` and complete the purchase there.
Do not use it to browse or discover all events in a category; TicketData exposes no list/browse API, so this CLI works from event ids and performer/venue slugs you already have or resolve by name.
Do not use it for primary on-sale ticket purchasing or seat selection; use the marketplace or box office.

recipes.title: Is this ticket cheap right now?
recipes.command: ticketdata-pp-cli watch add 22323960 && ticketdata-pp-cli sync && ticketdata-pp-cli stats 22323960 --agent
recipes.explanation: Track the event, pull its history, and see where today's get-in price sits in its own distribution.

recipes.title: Alert me when a floor drops to my price
recipes.explanation: Flags target hits and any floor that moved more than 8% since the last sync.

recipes.title: Which date is cheapest for this artist?
recipes.explanation: Ranks all of the performer's watched events by get-in price and percent change.

recipes.title: Narrow a large event payload for an agent
recipes.explanation: Pulls only the decision-relevant fields from the verbose event response using dotted select paths.

recipes.title: Find the best-value section
recipes.explanation: Ranks the event's zones by current get-in price and by each zone's drop versus its own history.

trigger_phrases: when should I buy tickets to
is this ticket cheap right now
